name: Garlic Chicken Wings
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate wings at joints; discard tips in a strong plastic bag.
2. Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet to 2 inches deep until hot.
3. Shake wings in seasoned flour and fry until golden brown and crisp on both sides.
4. Remove wings, drain on paper towels.
5. When all wings are done, remove all but 2 tablespoons of oil, leaving the browned bits on the bottom of the skillet.
6. Add finely chopped garlic to the oil in the skillet and sauté until soft, but not browned.
7. Add dry sherry into the skillet and scrape up the browned bits remaining.
8. Add chicken broth, stir, and reduce the sauce by one third. It will become a bit thicker.
9. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper; return wings to sauce to heat briefly, coating them with the sauce.
